correlation_id_references.annulus_single_phase: Kern-annulus single-phase (TASK-007 frozen registry entry, 1950-literature reference, with documented 5-10% correlation uncertainty band over the canonical 1"/2" tube-in-shell operating envelope; see _tolerance_metadata.json per_field_basis)
correlation_id_references.tube_single_phase: Gnielinski-tube single-phase (TASK-007 frozen registry entry, 1976-literature reference, with documented 5-10% correlation uncertainty band over the canonical operating envelope; see _tolerance_metadata.json per_field_basis)

provider_id_references.CoolProp: TASK-015A frozen registry entry (CoolProp property provider, documented < 0.5% numerical accuracy for water single-phase liquid at 273-373 K; basis for the per-field tolerances in _tolerance_metadata.json)

slice1_skeleton_note: Slice 1 records frozen-contract references for downstream Slice 2 wiring. TBD-by-Slice-2 base SHAs are placeholders that Slice 2 fills in from the actual frozen contract base SHAs at implementation time.
slice2_status: slice2-materialized: true; frozen contract SHAs computed via SHA-256 over concatenated UTF-8 contract file blobs (per compute_frozen_upstream_contract_versions in src/hexagent/validation_report/double_pipe_validation_report.py). TASK-019 amendment-001 freezes the case input / expected_output / tolerance vectors; the frozen contract SHAs are unchanged from Slice 2 closeout (PR #100 merge) and are reproduced verbatim below for auditability.
amendment_basis: TASK-019 amendment-001 freezes the case input vectors, the case expected output vectors for TASK-006/007/008/017/018-authorized fields, and the per-field tolerance values for all three TASK-019 Golden cases. No production calculation was executed; all numeric vectors are engineering-literature-referenced canonical baselines. The frozen contract SHAs below are unchanged from Slice 2 closeout and are reproduced for auditability; no upstream contract blob was mutated by this amendment.

case_source_basis.TASK-019-GOLDEN-01: Kern, D.Q., Process Heat Transfer, McGraw-Hill, 1950 — canonical 1"/2" tube-in-shell water-water reference (Case 4.2, p. 92). Geometry: tube OD 33.4 mm × tube ID 26.6 mm × tube length 2.0 m; shell ID 52.5 mm × shell OD 60.3 mm. Operating envelope: hot-side 333.15 K / cold-side 293.15 K, both 0.5 kg/s, both 101325 Pa inlet. Fouling 0.0002 m^2 K/W both sides. The frozen vectors in case_01_heat_balance_rating.json are derived from the documented heat-balance closure at this operating point.
case_source_basis.TASK-019-GOLDEN-02: Same geometry and fluid envelope as TASK-019-GOLDEN-01 with explicit material selection (SS304 per TASK-017 approved material catalog) and ASME BPVC Section VIII Div 1 design-code (per TASK-017 approved rule-pack). Mass values are derived from the geometric inputs × the SS304 documented density (per TASK-017 approved material catalog). Preliminary mechanical check PASSES because the design pressure (600000 Pa) and design temperature (343.15 K) are well below the SS304 allowable stress envelope at this temperature (per TASK-017 §9.1).
case_source_basis.TASK-019-GOLDEN-03: Same geometry and fluid envelope as TASK-019-GOLDEN-01 with the full TASK-018 Slice A / B / C outputs. Cost components are engineering-literature-referenced canonical baselines (RSMeans-like 2024 stainless-steel tubing + ASME labor) for the SS304 / ASME BPVC VIII-1 design-code combination, US-Midwest-Industrial-2024 region, USD currency, USD-CPI-2024 escalation rule (TASK-018 §6 frozen rule). Life-cycle energy summary is derived from heat_duty × annual_operating_hours × fouling_energy_penalty_factor with no discount / salvage formula (those remain TASK-018 §5.3 / §5.3.2 deferred amendments).

canonical_hashing_rule: Per frozen design §3.3 + MASTER_DEVELOPMENT_SPEC §15.3: canonical JSON (sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace, no NaN / Infinity, integers for minor units only — no float for money); SHA-256 lowercase hex, 64 characters; ISO 8601 UTC with explicit Z suffix. The canonical-JSON SHA-256 of the frozen input and expected_output blocks is computed by validation_report.materialize_case_block_from_fixture (src/hexagent/validation_report/double_pipe_validation_report.py) and surfaced as case_block.input_sha256 / case_block.expected_output_sha256.

The amendment-001 SHA will be re-computed by the Slice 3 / post-amendment-001 closeout round and is NOT frozen in this mutable design branch row per the P1 self-reference guard.
